summary.short_rationale: Ostrikov is high-signal but overlap-heavy around agentic development. His value is complementary depth: AI-first development process, PRD-to-agent workflows, agent loops, skills, context compaction, and practical architecture teardown.
summary.strongest_argument_for: The passport contains concrete, reusable operator patterns such as AI-optimized PRDs, simple REPL/agent loops, 3-level context compaction, and SKILL.md abstraction.
summary.strongest_argument_against: The accepted roster already has strong agentic workflow coverage, especially Glebkudr, Refat, Neuraldeep, AI Architect, and Ilia.

accepted_basis_cells.interpretation: primary admission basis as AI-first development-process depth
accepted only as overlap/depth against Glebkudr and the dense agent_ops rollup

good_fit: AI-optimized PRD structure for coding agents
agent loops, skills, context compaction, and tool execution architecture
simple agent architectures versus overcomplicated graph frameworks
hackathon and open-source coding-agent architecture lessons
poor_fit: source of record for RAG architecture

risk_notes: Compact corpus: 68 posts, though comments are active.
Some source depth may live in external videos rather than the text corpus.
Admission increases density in the agentic workflow cluster.
Needs routing caveat to avoid duplicate routing against Glebkudr, Refat, Neuraldeep, AI Architect, and Ilia.
recommended_manifest_caveat: accepted as complementary/depth expert for AI-first development process and practical agent architecture; avoid counting agent_ops overlap as a new gap

probe_shape: 1 RAG query where Ostrikov should remain supporting
